sourceMovie.title: Shooter
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Shooter (2007) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Antoine Fuqua's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Patriot Games
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Shooter clicked for you, Patriot Games (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Clear and Present Danger
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Shooter clicked for you, Clear and Present Danger (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Shooter?
faq.answer: Try Patriot Games, Clear and Present Danger, Sniper—each shares DNA with Shooter while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Shooter?
faq.answer: Shooter (2007) is best described as Drama, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Shooter worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
